pi_items.source: Verified in setup_task.sh (line 11 clears /home/ga/Downloads), export_result.sh (line 8 sets OUTPUT_PATH), and verifier.py (line 27 uses metadata value with same default)

pi_items.verified_value: Verifier checks for filenames containing: ("annual" AND "report" AND ".pdf") OR ("contract" AND "template" AND ".pdf"). Actual filenames are "Annual-Report-2023.pdf" and "Contract-Template.pdf" which match the verifier logic but not the stated required_content format.
pi_items.source: verifier.py lines 68 and 76 show hardcoded checks for these keywords in lowercase filenames, not in the metadata.required_content list which is never used

Test ZIP created with the two actual PDFs (annual_report_2023.pdf: 9.6K uncompressed, quarterly_report.pdf: 6.4K uncompressed renamed as Contract-Template.pdf) using ZIP_DEFLATED compression yields ~9KB ZIP file. The claimed 1000 bytes (1 KB) is significantly lower than the actual expected size.

pi_items.metadata_value: Annual Report 2023 and Contract Template are legitimate business documents
pi_items.verified_value: annual_report_2023.pdf contains actual ACME Corporation annual report with 'Annual Report 2023' title and financial data. quarterly_report.pdf (used as Contract-Template.pdf) contains Q3 2023 quarterly financial report, NOT contract or template information. The quarterly_report.pdf was verified to contain 'report' keyword but NOT 'contract' or 'template' keywords.
pi_items.source: PDF content extraction using PyPDF2 library on /workspace/data/annual_report_2023.pdf and /workspace/data/quarterly_report.pdf. setup_nuxeo.sh lines 125-140 show that quarterly_report.pdf is copied and renamed as Contract_Template.pdf.

pi_items.metadata_value: Not explicitly stated in metadata, but task description implies 'Annual Report 2023' and 'Contract Template'
pi_items.verified_value: In Nuxeo setup (setup_nuxeo.sh lines 217-230): Document is uploaded with name 'Annual-Report-2023' to /default-domain/workspaces/Projects/ with title 'Annual Report 2023', and name 'Contract-Template' to /default-domain/workspaces/Templates/ with title 'Contract Template'
pi_items.source: setup_nuxeo.sh lines 217-230 and verifier.py lines 68,76 which check for these names in filenames with 'annual', 'report', 'contract', 'template' keywords
Task description explicitly states 'Save the downloaded file to /home/ga/Downloads/vendor_package.zip'. export_result.sh line 8 checks for this exact path.

pi_items.metadata_value: ZIP must contain files matching 'annual' AND 'report' AND '.pdf' patterns and 'contract' AND 'template' AND '.pdf' patterns (case-insensitive)
pi_items.verified_value: Based on verifier.py lines 68-81: Needs valid ZIP file (20 points), Annual Report match (35 points), Contract Template match (35 points), file created during task (10 points). Total 90+ points required to pass. The metadata.required_content list is never used by the verifier.
pi_items.source: verifier.py lines 58-91 show explicit scoring logic and filename pattern checks

privileged_info_summary: The task 'bundle_download_documents' requires downloading two PDF documents from a Nuxeo platform and saving them as a ZIP archive to /home/ga/Downloads/vendor_package.zip. The two documents are: (1) Annual Report 2023 from the Projects workspace, stored as annual_report_2023.pdf (9.6K, real ACME Corporation annual report), and (2) Contract Template from the Templates workspace, actually stored as quarterly_report.pdf (6.4K, Q3 financial report, not a contract). When downloaded as a ZIP, the files would be named Annual-Report-2023.pdf and Contract-Template.pdf. The verifier checks that the ZIP contains valid files with filenames matching these patterns (case-insensitive keywords). The actual ZIP file size would be approximately 9KB, not the claimed minimum of 1000 bytes. The metadata contains contradictions: the required_content field is not used by the verifier, the min_zip_size_bytes claim is significantly underestimated, and the Contract Template document is actually a quarterly report mislabeled in setup.
